pipeline_components.architect: EdogQaLlmClient.ArchitectOnceAsync (gpt-5.4, high reasoning, strict json_schema, T1g prompt: COVERAGE BREADTH + EVIDENCE LINE PRECISION + DEFENSIVE CODE BIAS)
pipeline_components.editor: EdogQaLlmClient.EditorOnceAsync (gpt-5.4-mini, low reasoning, strict json_schema, T1g prompt: VERB SELECTION GUIDE matcher-tied + CATEGORY SELECTION GUIDE)
pipeline_components.scorer: tests/qa-eval/score_eval.py --span-expansion 5 (T1i: hunk-bounded forward expansion, 2-tier overlap tiebreaker)

notes: T1i (deterministic span-expansion scorer): macro recall lifted 0.542 -> 0.583 (+0.042 absolute, +7.7% relative), macro precision_highest_stage 0.613 -> 0.661 (+0.048 absolute, +7.8% relative), F1_highest 0.574 -> 0.619 (+0.045). PR-975848 0.625 -> 0.750 (+0.125, gained s01 HappyPath FieldMatch sum-to-1.0 invariant via expansion of sk-1 anchor [185, 190] -> effective span hitting curator-expected [193-198]). PR-976609 and PR-977882 unchanged at N=5; the 2-tier overlap tiebreaker (prefer-original-line-overlap) prevented greedy-reallocation pair theft that would otherwise occur at N>=7 on PR-977882. EdogQaLlmClient.cs is unchanged (byte-identical to T1g commit 0cf3903); the lift comes entirely from scorer-side normalization of the architect block-entry anchoring bias against the unified-diff hunk geometry. No paid LLM capture was required for T1i. Remaining headroom toward the spec nominal 0.80 lives in (a) global bipartite matching to unlock larger N safely (T1j), and (b) curator augmentation of the 3-PR corpus toward 8-10 PRs (T1k). precision_validated remains 0.0 across the corpus because the scorer uses the validated-stage bucket which is structurally empty under our highest-stage tagging. See score_report.json schema 1.2 for the immutable scored snapshot.
